env.sglang: v0.5.12 + RDNA4 patches (incl. 041 AWQ GEMV, 042 FP8 reclaim)

infra_fix: iommu=pt cleared the post-131K-prefill TP2 NCCL channel-renegotiation wedge: NCCL log lines 178278 -> 4 (events ceased, not just logging), no-spec 131K decode 0.68 -> 16.83 tok/s. Short-context P2P never tripped it (why the 97 tok/s short-ctx runs missed it).
method: single-user decode tok/s = 1000 / Mean_TPOT_ms via sglang.bench_serving (random dataset); spec-decode also measured on coherent coding prompts. context label = bench random-input length (repo convention input=ctx/2).

quality.basic: PASS (coherent code under no-spec and spec)

Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-FP8.notes: no-spec long-context curve + EAGLE3 coherent ~86 tok/s are post-iommu=pt real data; pilot pre-fix no-spec 256K (0.68) and spec (2.56/72) DISCARDED. accept_len 5.5 confirms topk16 tree active (chain would be ~2-3); the pilot's 'tree kernel unavailable/greedy' message did NOT recur. 256K random spec 2.44 is a no-wedge sanity check only (random tokens => ~0 draft acceptance), not coherent throughput.

validate.raw: 3/3; basic='paris', thinking 446tok finish=stop, vision saw red/circle/round

spec.ctx_ceiling: RESOLVED 2026-05-31 via --chunked-prefill 2048 @mem0.85: 253K-tok prefill serves, ~45 tok/s coherent, accept ~3.9, max_total_num_tokens=782523 (KV pool was NEVER the limit). The earlier 'OOMs at every mem-fraction' was a prefill-transient (gdn_backend/fused-MoE per-chunk), NOT a draft+verify memory wall — cp2048 shrinks the per-chunk transient. launch.sh auto-applies cp2048 for qwen36-moe FP8 when spec-decode is on. So FP8+DFlash@256K works (2nd FP8+draft@256K model alongside Coder-30B EAGLE3).

Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8.notes: FP8 no-spec is the 256K-capable path (1.6M-tok KV, 17-18 tok/s, 3/3 caps). DFlash reaches 256K (~45 tok/s coherent, accept ~3.9) via --chunked-prefill 2048 — CORRECTION: the earlier 'NOT 256K-capable / OOMs at every mem-fraction' was wrong; the OOM was a prefill-transient (gdn_backend/fused-MoE per-chunk), not a draft+verify memory wall (KV pool 782K). cp2048 shrinks the per-chunk transient → 253K-tok prefill serves. So FP8+DFlash@256K works (2nd FP8+draft@256K model). AWQ-int4 is still faster absolute (80) but FP8 now reaches the same 256K.

spec.note: no spec path (MistralForCausalLMEagle would load a SpecForge-trained draft; none exists for Devstral)

Devstral-Small-2-24B-FP8.notes: At devstral2 defaults (mem 0.90, fp8 KV) KV pool tops out at max_total=180572 < 262144 — a single sequence CANNOT reach full 256K; largest validated IN=131072. CONTRADICTS the devstral2 preset comment ('patch-042 reclaim -> 413K -> full 256K') — that figure was likely the v1 dense Devstral-24B-FP8 (no vision tower) or a pre-vision build; v2 carries a BF16 vision tower that eats the KV budget. FOLLOW-UP (#10): retest at mem 0.92 to see if 256K single-seq is reachable (documented +22% lever). basic+vision+tool all PASS; no draft.

needle_241k.note: coherent decode near 256K, not just KV alloc

spec.note: no SpecForge draft for Devstral; same as FP8

validate.vision: PASS (genuinely correct: 'white background with a large red circle' — not fabricated)

Devstral-Small-2-24B-AWQ.notes: AWQ int4 is the dense-VL 256K path for Devstral-2. int4 weights (~half FP8 bytes) free enough VRAM that the 507683-token KV pool clears full 262144 (256K) WITH the BF16 vision tower resident — vs FP8's 180572 cap. RESOLVES FP8 follow-up #10: the FP8 cap is WEIGHT-SIZE bound (patch-042 reclaim underdelivers because FP8 weights + BF16 vision leave <13 GB for KV); raising mem-fraction does not help, switching to AWQ does. ~24 tok/s short decode, gentle bandwidth-bound curve (20.8@32K, 18.2@65K). All capabilities preserved. Built from official FP8 via dequant_fp8_to_bf16.py -> code+vision AWQ calibration. Ships mattbucci/Devstral-Small-2-24B-AWQ.

validate.raw: 3/3 in 29.4s; basic=paris, thinking 520tok terminated, vision saw red/circle/round

gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-FP8.ctx_ceiling: RESOLVED 2026-05-31 — reaches its native 256K (max_position 262144) on TRITON flash attention (validated long prefill: FP8 87K-tok + AWQ 110K-tok, needle retrieved, no OOM, ~16.5 tok/s, image+thinking preserved; full 256K serve confirmed via triton SWA). The ~32-64K cap was torch_native-ONLY: ROCm has only the MATH SDPA backend, so Gemma's GLOBAL (full-attn) layers materialize chunk_q x full_kv per chunk -> >9GB transient -> OOM. The 'Triton attn crashes with SWA' note was the PRE-001 state; 001's SWA-aware triton path (swa_attn_logits + HybridLinearKVPool layers_mapping/translate_loc) + the triton preset default fix it. gemma4 preset now defaults to triton.
gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-FP8.bug_found: torch_native + SWA: chunked_prefill_size(4096) > sliding_window(1024) crashes at torch_native_backend.py:104 on first long chunked prefill (redundant-query buffer capped to window). WORKAROUND --chunked-prefill 1024; gemma4 preset should set CHUNKED<=1024 for long ctx.
gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-FP8.notes: CORRECTS README FP8 table 'gemma-4-26B-A4B FP8 256K, 18.7 tok/s': 290K is the KV POOL, but a single sequence can't prefill past ~32-64K on torch_native; 18.7 was short-ctx decode (measured 9.89 @32K). NOT 256K-capable on this backend. fp8 native confirmed (fp8_w8a8, per_channel, fp8_e4m3 KV). FOLLOW-UP: same torch_native root cause as Nemotron-Omni below — triton flash attention is the fix; gemma4's separate SWA-triton crash is a distinct bug to chase (like patch 047 chased Nemotron's hybrid-pool triton crash).

Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-FP8.kind: Mamba2-Transformer hybrid MoE (128e/6 active), AVLM (text+image+video+audio) + thinking, NVIDIA ModelOpt per-tensor FP8

validate.needle_247k: INCONCLUSIVE — serving is fine (no OOM/crash, coherent) but the reasoning model exhausts its thinking budget at extreme depth; retrieval-quality, not a serving issue

spec.note: 0 mtp/eagle tensors in ckpt (19353 total); Nano excluded from MTP per Nemotron-3 white paper; none bundled or published/transferable

Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-FP8.unblock: patches 046 + 047. 046 (SSD divergent-ptr buffer-ops): ssd_chunk_scan _chunk_scan_fwd_kernel + ssd_chunk_state _chunk_state_varlen_kernel reassigned a state pointer to a DIFFERENT base tensor (states vs initstates) inside an scf.if -> AMD TritonAMDGPUCanonicalizePointers abort (fatPtrs canNarrow) the moment initial_states appears (chunked prefill) -> ~8K cap; fixed BIT-EXACT via single-based ptrs + tl.where value-select, buffer ops stay ON. 047 (triton attn hybrid v_head_dim): TritonAttnBackend get_value_buffer(0) failed because layer 0 is a Mamba layer (full-attn layers sparse at [5,12,19,26,33,42]) -> ValueError -> forced torch_native; fixed by duck-typing HybridLinearKVPool.get_v_head_dim().
Nemotron-3-Nano-Omni-30B-A3B-FP8.notes: FULL 256K via TRITON flash attention — memory-efficient (247K-tok prefill, full-token-usage 0.07, NO OOM, ~109s) AND fast (~29 tok/s decode at 247K; Mamba2 hybrid stays fast at long ctx, faster than the dense/DeltaNet FP8 models). torch_native is the correct-but-slow fallback: ROCm has only the MATH SDPA backend (flash/mem-efficient report 'No available kernel') -> materializes O(chunk x ctx) scores -> OOM past ~150K, ~140 tok/s prefill. launch.sh nemotron-omni defaults to triton + CTX 262144, no memory workarounds (default mem 0.85 / cp 4096). The earlier '~8K ceiling / Triton-AMD chunk-scan compiler bug' verdict is SUPERSEDED. First Mamba2 hybrid on the box.
